────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  LOOP ON YOUR REPO
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  repo : ~/your-project
  test : python -m pytest -q tests/test_cart.py
  file : src/cart.py
  goal : discount_pct is a percentage; 10 means 10% off

  @LoopArchitect: this is a bugfix loop — exit gate is YOUR test command, max
  3 revisions, RivalReviewer reads each real run.

  ┃ QAEngineer ▸ revision 0: `python -m pytest -q tests/test_cart.py` → ❌
  ┃ RivalReviewer ▸ still failing:
      E        +  where -900.0 = total([('x', 100.0)], 10)
      
      tests/test_cart.py:10: AssertionError
      =========================== short test summary info ============================
      FAILED tests/test_cart.py::test_ten_percent - AssertionError: assert -900.0 =...
      1 failed, 1 passed in 0.03s
  ┃ CodeAuthor ▸ patching src/cart.py against the real failure…

  ┃ QAEngineer ▸ revision 1: `python -m pytest -q tests/test_cart.py` → ✅

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  LOOP COMPLETE — your tests pass
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  RivalReviewer: ran your real suite, it's green. Shipping.
  status: shipped after 1 revision(s)
caption: A real run of `run_on_repo.py` — captured, not mocked. The model's fix is gated by the repo's own pytest.
